reasoning:
The informal statement asks to show that sin(π/12) is an algebraic number. In standard mathematical terminology, "algebraic number" means algebraic over ℚ, i.e., a root of some nonzero polynomial with rational coefficients. The Lean statement `IsAlgebraic ℚ (sin (Real.pi/12))` uses Mathlib's `IsAlgebraic` definition, which is exactly `∃ p : R[X], p ≠ 0 ∧ aeval x p = 0`, with R = ℚ here. This precisely captures "sin(π/12) is algebraic over ℚ," so the formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.</reasoning>
